title: cerastium vulgatum var. tianschanicum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum var. tianschanicum, also known as the common mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species native to Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Cerastium vulgatum var. tianschanicum is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also added to salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
